summary: Frontend-only testing of the Apex Stage Riggers 3D landing page completed. All 11 requested test cases pass: WebGL hero canvas renders without errors, Rig Control panel (Lights/Show toggles + truss height slider) is fully functional, all sections (Services x5, Work x4, About, Quote) render correctly, all external images (Unsplash + Pexels) return HTTP 200 and load in-browser, navbar/hero anchor links scroll to correct sections, and quote form validation + success flow (sonner toasts + status text) works as designed. No backend testing performed (per instructions).
critical_code_review_comments: Landing.jsx is 650+ lines; consider splitting Navbar / Hero / Services / Work / About / Quote / Footer into separate files under src/components/landing/ for maintainability.
Quote form has no client-side email format validation beyond input type='email' — empty/invalid emails currently only blocked by required-field check (acceptable for prototype).
StageScene.jsx uses OrbitControls autoRotate; consider disabling autoRotate while user interacts to avoid camera fighting drag — minor UX nicety.
Helmet/SEO tags missing (no <title>, meta description) — fine for prototype.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend-only prototype, no backend involvement. Quote form is intentionally client-side (sonner toast + local 'submitted' state). Truss height slider is a controlled React range input — when testing programmatically use keyboard ArrowLeft/Right or the native HTMLInputElement value setter then dispatch 'input' event; setting el.value directly will NOT propagate to React state. All Unsplash/Pexels images load successfully. WebGL/three.js scene mounts without console errors in headless Chromium.

verified_behaviours.truss_slider: 7.0 m -> 6.0 m -> 8.0 m display updates correctly
verified_behaviours.success_form_toast: Quote request received — we'll be in touch.
